ordinal_rationale: parent r4 impl ω-cycle stamped ω+1 (operates on Bellman fixpoint by mutating DAG). this deepening operates on the rollout-policy choice within the ω+1 mutation step — one transfinite step above. ω+2 < ε_0 still holds (Gentzen ordinal envelope intact); r19 Gödel/self-cert escape preserved.
rule: raw:design-engine-deepening-omega-cycle (hive raw 47 + raw 70 + raw 71 falsifier mandatory) — deepening of parent Tier-1 i1 axis
subject: MCTS rollout policy for schedule_replan() — UCB1 c=√2, depth ≤ 3, rollouts ≤ 32, with tree expansion + backpropagation. Resolves how leaves are expanded (uniform vs prior-weighted), how rollouts terminate, and how transposition / memoization interact with the bounded budget.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'deepening ω-cycle for r4.i1 mcts-rollout-policy — Track-A ≥7 / Track-B ≥6, witness JSON only, no code'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, deepening of parent i1. Track-A (≥7 final-form rollout-policy axes) = positive design ceilings (default policy, memoization, transposition, termination, virtual-loss, r2 cross-feed, deterministic seed). Track-B (≥6 limits) = negative design ceilings (l5 UCB regret, l1 NFL, l2 halting, transposition memory unbounded, l6 no-PAC, l7 Cantor 4th strategy). dedup → cluster → Tier ≤ 4. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint where additional Track-A axes are absorbed by existing Track-B limits + concrete falsifier (geo-mean T*_post/T*_pre < 1−ε on N≥5 inherited from parent i5 benchmark) re-anchored at the rollout-policy level.
failure_modes_addressed: shallow rollout — depth 3 may be too short to reach leaves where reward differentiates strategies
exploration vs exploitation imbalance — c=√2 textbook default may be wrong on roadmap domain
rollout policy bias — which strategy to try first influences early UCB statistics disproportionately at n=32

axes_surfaced.name: default_rollout_policy_uniform_vs_prior
axes_surfaced.summary: default expansion policy at unexpanded leaves: uniform-random over deltas from {soft_drop, parallel_split, seed_inject} vs prior-weighted by per-strategy historical success (hooks i6 strategy_weight_learning). default = UNIFORM RANDOM for r4 v0 — honesty under l1 NFL, no roadmap-domain prior assumed without benchmark evidence. prior-weighted reserved for r4 v1 after ≥16 outcomes per strategy logged. expand_default(leaf, deltas) returns deltas[rand(0, len(deltas))] with deterministic seed (a7).

axes_surfaced.name: tree_node_memoization_within_budget
axes_surfaced.summary: within a single schedule_replan call (budget = 32 rollouts), memoize node expansions: if leaf state s already has expanded children from a sibling rollout, reuse them instead of re-deriving deltas. memo key = (rm_hash, depth). bounded by budget so memory ≤ |M|^3 ≤ ~1KB per call. clears at function exit. saves ~30% wall-clock on bushy trees per back-of-envelope (32 rollouts × avg 2 reused expansions).

axes_surfaced.name: transposition_table_DAG_paths
axes_surfaced.summary: the search space is a DAG (multiple delta sequences may yield same rm_state). transposition table maps rm_state_hash → MCTS node, so distinct paths converge on shared statistics. concretely: applying [soft_drop(e1), parallel_split(n2)] vs [parallel_split(n2), soft_drop(e1)] reaches the same rm. table is intra-call only (cleared after schedule_replan returns) to bound memory under budget envelope (i3). if memory exceeds 4KB during a call, fall back to tree-only (no transposition) — see l_b3 limit.

axes_surfaced.name: simulation_termination_criterion
axes_surfaced.summary: rollout terminates when (a) depth == 3 (parent dossier hard cap), OR (b) no more deltas applicable (terminal state), OR (c) reward already saturates at +1 (T*_post ≤ T_∞ within float epsilon — Brent floor reached, no further improvement possible per l4). full-schedule simulation NOT used — would re-run schedule(rm2) at every leaf which is too costly. instead, reward = (T*_pre − T*_post)/T*_pre clipped to [-1, +1] computed once at terminal. depth cap dominates by design (raw 71: budget envelope is the falsifiable cap, not a heuristic).

axes_surfaced.name: virtual_loss_for_parallel_rollouts
axes_surfaced.summary: if r4 v1 parallelizes rollouts (k workers), virtual-loss decrements visit-count temporarily on a node being explored to prevent k workers from picking the same arm. r4 v0 is sequential (k=1), so virtual-loss is INERT in v0 — flagged for v1 only. interface: node.virtual_visits separate from node.real_visits; UCB1 uses (real_visits + virtual_visits). degenerate to standard UCB1 when k=1.

axes_surfaced.name: r2_bayesian_eta_during_rollout
axes_surfaced.summary: during MCTS rollout, schedule(rm2) currently uses point-estimate η in c_exp. r2 dossier provides p(η|history). per-rollout Thompson sample η ~ posterior, propagate through schedule(rm2) to compute reward. variance grows but expected reward unchanged; reduces over-confidence in single-η reward. integration point: schedule_replan inner loop calls sample_eta_posterior() before reward computation. NOT a pre-req for r4 v0 (parent i7 = Tier-2 backlog); flagged here as a1's natural extension into v1.

axes_surfaced.name: deterministic_seed_for_reproducibility
axes_surfaced.summary: all randomness in expand_default (a1), uniform random selection, and r2 Thompson sample (a6) seeded by hash(rm_state || trigger_ts || rollout_id). same input → same MCTS tree → same chosen delta. critical for falsifier reproducibility (raw 71): benchmark suite (parent i5) must be re-runnable with bit-identical results. seed exposed as schedule_replan(rm, seed=None) — None means hash(rm || ts) auto-derive.

axes_surfaced.name: progressive_widening_alternative
axes_surfaced.summary: alternative to fixed-arm UCB1: progressive widening k_n = ceil(C·n^α), α=0.5, expanding new arms only as visit count grows. relevant when |deltas| > 32 (more arms than rollouts). r4 v0 has |deltas| ≤ ~8 typically (3 strategies × few candidates each), so progressive widening is INERT. flagged as v1 axis if delta-pool grows beyond budget. parent i1 dossier mentions this as alternative; here documented as not-yet-needed.

axes_surfaced.name: l5_ucb_regret_finite_suboptimality
axes_surfaced.summary: Auer-Cesa-Bianchi-Fischer 2002: R_n ≤ O((log n)/Δ²). at n=32, log(32) ≈ 3.47; for Δ=0.1, regret ≤ ~347 units in raw scale (normalized to bounded-reward setting yields finite but nonzero regret). means: chosen rollout policy CANNOT be ε-optimal for arbitrary ε; finite suboptimality permanent. impacts a1 default-policy choice — uniform random gives unbiased but high-variance reward estimate; prior-weighted reduces variance but introduces bias (l1). cannot escape the regret floor.

axes_surfaced.name: l1_nfl_rollout_policy_problem_specific
axes_surfaced.summary: Wolpert-Macready 1997 NFL: averaged over all reward landscapes, no rollout policy beats uniform random. a1 prior-weighted (a6 r2 Thompson, a8 progressive widening) all presuppose roadmap-domain structure. on adversarial DAGs the prior is wrong and prior-weighted underperforms uniform. r4 v0 default = uniform random is the NFL-honest choice. rollout policy is provably problem-specific.

axes_surfaced.name: l2_halting_rollout_depth_fixed
axes_surfaced.summary: r12 halting: cannot determine in general whether deeper rollout (depth > 3) would yield strictly better reward. infinite-depth MCTS impossible — no termination guarantee on arbitrary mutation operators. r4 sidesteps via depth ≤ 3 hard cap (a4 termination criterion (a)). claim is finite-horizon optimal-within-budget, NOT asymptotic optimum. shallow-rollout failure mode is mitigated by depth-cap honesty, not by deeper search.

axes_surfaced.name: transposition_table_memory_unbounded_worst_case
axes_surfaced.summary: a3 transposition table size in worst case is |reachable_states| which can be |M|^depth before sharing. on adversarial DAG with no actual collisions (every path yields distinct rm_state_hash), table grows to full tree size with no benefit. mitigation: hard memory cap (4KB per call) + fallback to tree-only when exceeded (a3). cannot eliminate worst-case unboundedness; only bound it by the same i3 budget envelope that bounds the tree.

axes_surfaced.name: l6_deterministic_vs_stochastic_no_pac
axes_surfaced.summary: a7 deterministic seed makes runs reproducible but provides NO PAC (Probably Approximately Correct) guarantee — same as l6 from parent. PAC requires sample-size n ≥ poly(1/ε, 1/δ, |H|); deterministic n=32 fixed. stochastic alternative (Thompson sampling a6 with non-deterministic seed) provides Bayesian regret bound but still not frequentist PAC. tradeoff: reproducibility (deterministic) vs adaptive coverage (stochastic). r4 v0 picks deterministic for falsifier integrity; sacrifices PAC.

axes_surfaced.name: cantor_fourth_strategy_always_possible
axes_surfaced.summary: l7 from parent re-anchored at rollout-policy level: for any finite k-policy enumeration {uniform, prior-weighted, progressive-widening, Thompson, ...}, ∃ DAG family where optimal policy is a (k+1)-th not in the pool. enumeration of rollout policies is provably non-exhaustive. mitigation: clean policy registry (expand_default trait/interface) so adding a 5th policy is mechanical. inherits non-exhaustion from parent l7 by Cantor diagonal.

dedupe_method: (1) a1 default-policy vs a6 r2-Thompson: a1 selects which delta to try, a6 samples η for reward computation — orthogonal layers (arm selection vs reward estimation). (2) a2 memoization vs a3 transposition: a2 reuses children list (delta enumeration), a3 reuses node statistics (visit/reward) — distinct caches, both intra-call. (3) a4 termination vs b3 halting: a4 is the implementation of the b3 Lyapunov surrogate — paired (final-form ↔ limit anchor). (4) a5 virtual-loss vs a6 Thompson: virtual-loss handles parallelism in arm selection, Thompson handles uncertainty in reward — orthogonal in the parallel-MCTS literature (Chaslot et al. 2008). (5) a7 deterministic vs b5 no-PAC: a7 chooses determinism, b5 documents that determinism does not buy PAC — final-form ↔ limit pairing. (6) a8 progressive-widening vs a1 default-policy: a8 modifies arm-pool growth, a1 modifies arm-selection within fixed pool — orthogonal. cluster: A=arm-selection (a1/a6/a8), B=tree-engineering (a2/a3/a5), C=termination-and-reproducibility (a4/a7), D=regret-and-NFL (b1/b2), E=halting-and-memory (b3/b4), F=PAC-and-Cantor (b5/b6). 6 clusters mirror parent's 6.

tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.theory_anchor: b1 l5 UCB regret O(log n / Δ²) — uniform random is unbiased default; b2 l1 NFL — biased policy only after benchmark confirms domain prior
tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.falsifier: with uniform-random expand_default(), geo-mean T*_post/T*_pre on parent i5 benchmark suite must already meet ε=0.01 bar. if uniform-random fails the bar but prior-weighted passes, a1's default choice is falsified — escalate to a v1-promotion of prior-weighted via i6 weight-learning.

tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.theory_anchor: b3 l2 halting — depth-cap is Lyapunov surrogate; r15 Brent (parent l4) — reward saturation at T_∞ floor
tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.falsifier: log {rollout_id, terminal_reason ∈ {depth_cap, no_deltas, reward_saturated}, depth_reached} per rollout. assert depth_reached ≤ 3 across all benchmarks. if any rollout reaches depth > 3 OR if depth_cap reason exceeds 95% (suggesting depth too short to differentiate strategies), axis is falsified.

tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.theory_anchor: raw 71 falsifier mandatory — benchmark must be bit-reproducible; b5 l6 no-PAC documented limit
tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.falsifier: running schedule_replan(rm, seed=42) twice on identical rm must produce bit-identical chosen delta and reward sequence. if any run differs, axis is falsified. additionally, parent i5 benchmark harness must be re-runnable with bit-identical geo-mean T*_post/T*_pre; if not, falsifier integrity is broken.

tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.theory_anchor: DAG = multiple paths to same state (graph-theoretic); b4 memory-unbounded-worst-case forces hard cap
tier_1_immediate_design_this_cycle.falsifier: on a benchmark DAG with known transposition (e.g., parent i5 category (d) compound), transposition table must record ≥1 hit per schedule_replan call. if zero hits across all benchmarks, table provides no benefit and axis is falsified — revert to tree-only. additionally, peak memory per call must stay ≤ 4KB; if exceeded, fallback (tree-only) must engage and be logged.

tier_1_loc_vs_parent: parent i1 estimated 50 LoC; Tier-1 sum here = 15+10+8+17 = 50 LoC, exactly matches parent budget. no drift.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: micro-optimization (~30% wall-clock) — benefit only measurable after a1/a3/a4 land and we observe re-derivation cost. defer until r4 v0 profiling shows expand_default is hot path.
requires r2 ω-cycle implementation (parent Tier-2 i7). cross-axis fusion only after r2 v0 ships.

tier_3_defer_or_split.reason: inert in r4 v0 (sequential, k=1). non-architectural — adding when k>1 ships is straightforward. defer to r4 v2 parallel-rollouts cycle.
inert when |deltas| ≤ ~8 (current strategy pool). becomes relevant only if delta-pool grows beyond budget (>32). defer until parent l7 Cantor 4th-strategy materializes and pool expands.

limit_axes_documented.label: UCB1 finite-time regret O(log n / Δ²)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: at n=32 with 3 strategies, regret bounded but never zero — finite suboptimality permanent
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: a1 unbiased uniform default; future a6 Thompson reduces variance; cannot eliminate floor

limit_axes_documented.label: NFL — rollout policy is problem-specific
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: prior-weighted policy presupposes roadmap-domain structure; on adversarial DAG, uniform is best
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: a1 default = uniform random for r4 v0; prior-weighted only after parent i6 logs ≥16 outcomes per strategy

limit_axes_documented.label: halting — rollout depth fixed, infinite-depth MCTS impossible
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Turing 1936 → undecidable termination on general dynamical system
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: cannot prove deeper rollout yields strictly better reward in general
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: a4 hard depth cap = 3; reward computed at terminal; finite-horizon claim only

limit_axes_documented.label: transposition table memory unbounded in worst case
limit_axes_documented.theorem: DAG reachable-state-space size = |M|^depth in non-collision worst case
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: on adversarial DAG with no transpositions, table grows with no benefit
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: a3 hard memory cap 4KB per call + tree-only fallback when exceeded

limit_axes_documented.label: deterministic vs stochastic — no PAC under fixed budget
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Valiant 1984 PAC framework — fixed-n cannot meet arbitrary (ε,δ)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: a7 determinism gives reproducibility but no PAC; stochastic gives Bayesian regret but no frequentist PAC
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: r4 v0 picks deterministic for falsifier integrity (raw 71); empirical falsifier replaces theoretical PAC bound

limit_axes_documented.label: Cantor — always 4th rollout policy possible
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Cantor diagonal — for any finite k-enumeration, ∃ instance optimal at k+1
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: 3-policy pool {uniform, prior-weighted, Thompson} is provably non-exhaustive
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: clean policy-registry interface so adding a 5th policy is mechanical, not architectural

falsifier_concrete.statement: r4.i1 v0 rollout-policy is falsified iff, on parent i5 benchmark suite (N≥5 DAGs in design/roadmap_engine/r4_bench/), the geometric mean of T*_post / T*_pre under uniform-random expand_default() ≥ 1 − ε for ε = 0.01, OR if any rollout exceeds depth 3 / wall-clock 2s / rollout-count 32, OR if seed=42 reruns yield non-bit-identical outputs, OR if transposition table records zero hits on the compound benchmark category (d).
falsifier_concrete.metric: geometric mean of T*_post / T*_pre under uniform-random rollout policy
falsifier_concrete.reproducibility_check: schedule_replan(rm, seed=42) bit-identical on rerun across all N benchmarks
falsifier_concrete.transposition_check: compound benchmark category (d) records ≥1 transposition hit per schedule_replan call
falsifier_concrete.harness_status: to-be-built as part of parent i5

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: 본 trawl 자체 — r4.i1 rollout-policy 의 sub-axis 를 enumerate (8 final-form + 6 limit)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: Track-A rollout-policy ↔ Track-B limit 직교 8+6 axis + 6 cluster joint (parent 의 6 cluster mirror)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: geo-mean T*_post/T*_pre < 1−ε on N≥5 (parent i5 inherited) + reproducibility (a7 seed) + budget (a4 depth/count/wall) + transposition hit-rate (a3 category-d). l5 UCB regret (b1) 가 a1 default-policy 의 unbiased-choice 정당화. l1 NFL (b2) 가 a1 uniform 결정의 honesty anchor.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: events JSONL 의 replan_attempt record 에 rollout-level 필드 확장: {rollout_id, terminal_reason, depth_reached, transposition_hits, seed} — append-only audit trail.

cross_cycle_coupling.with: parent r4 impl ω-cycle 2026-04-26_r4_impl_omega_cycle (Tier-1 i1 deepening)
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_axis: i1 → a1..a8 (positive rollout-policy unfold), i1 → b1..b6 (negative rollout-policy ceiling). b1=parent l5, b2=parent l1, b3=parent l2, b6=parent l7 (re-anchored at rollout-policy level)
cross_cycle_coupling.next_target: r4.i2 ucb1-c-tuning-per-strategy deepening — variance measurement methodology after a1 uniform-random landed
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: ω+2 ordinal stamp (deepening above parent ω+1); r4 v1 가 r2/r6 합쳐지면 ω+ω = ω·2 < ε_0 미만

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 8 rollout-policy axes (a1..a8) + Track-B 6 limit axes (b1..b6). second-pass 신규 0. 각 limit axis 가 명시된 lower-bound 정리 인용. final-form axis 가 limit axis 와 1:1 또는 N:1 anchor (a1 ↔ b1+b2, a4 ↔ b3, a3 ↔ b4, a7 ↔ b5, a8 ↔ b6). concrete falsifier (geo-mean T*_post/T*_pre < 1−ε on N≥5 + reproducibility + budget + transposition-hit) 이 raw 71 mandatory 충족.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT — Tier-1 4 rollout-policy axes (a1/a4/a7/a3 / loc 50, exactly matches parent i1 budget) / Tier-2 2 (a2/a6 cross-axis defer) / Tier-3 2 (a5/a8 inert in v0) / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 6 (theorem 인용 완료) / Falsifier 1 (geo-mean N≥5 strict improvement + reproducibility + budget + transposition).
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: Track-A 추가 시 Track-B limit 중 하나가 cap 으로 감지됨 (예: 9번째 final-form axis 후보 'rollout_pruning_alpha_beta' → b3 halting 으로 흡수 since pruning에 termination guarantee 없음; 'reward_shaping_per_strategy' → b2 NFL 로 흡수 since strategy-prior 임). 더 추가하려는 axis 가 모두 기존 limit 으로 흡수됨 = enumeration 포화. 추가로 Tier-1 sum LoC 50 = parent i1 estimate 50 정확 일치 — 더 이상 LoC 확장 없이 enumerate 종료.
omega_stop_witness.ordinal_stamp_check: ω+2 < ε_0 — Gentzen ordinal 인용 가능, r19 self-cert 회피 유지. parent ω+1 위 한 단계 deepening 으로 정합.

first_design_target.name: a1 uniform-random expand_default + a4 depth-cap termination
first_design_target.rationale: a1/a4 는 다른 모든 rollout-policy axis 의 안전망 (default policy 없으면 leaf expansion 미정의, depth cap 없으면 a3 transposition 도 무한 루프 가능). 둘 합쳐 25 LoC, 가장 가벼운 진입점 — 부모 i3/i4 (budget+atomicity) 가 먼저 land 한 후 즉시 빌드 가능.
first_design_target.next_after: a7 deterministic seed (a1 uniform-random 의 reproducibility 담보) → a3 transposition table (메모리 cap 과 hit-rate 측정) → a2/a6 (Tier-2 cross-axis) → a5/a8 (Tier-3 v1+ defer)
